metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4102
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 6A-F: Immunogold staining in synaptosomal preparations from an adult rat revealed syntaphilin to be localized to the presynaptic plasma membrane. Quantification of syntaphilin immunoreactive puncta in preparations derived from the adult brain demonstrated a relative distribution of 38.7±3.1% adherent to the presynaptic plasma membrane and 14.5±2.6% adherent to synaptic vesicular structures.
Fig6G: Immunogold staining without prior incubation with syntaphilin antibody demonstrated no labelling.
Fig6H: Immunoblot analysis of total rat brain homogenate, synaptosomes, synaptic plasma membrane, and brain mitochondrial extracts generated by differential centrifugation with an antibody against syntaphilin showed enhanced signal in the synaptic plasma membrane fraction. Na+/K+-ATPase was used as a marker for the synaptic plasma membrane.

18/01/2021 Dnyanada: Additional literature characterised the anti-syntaphilin used in the study. Authors verify the specificity of the anti-Syntaphilin antibody by lack of staining when preincubated with Syntaphilin-fusion protein. However, the data isn't shown.
The brain region/synapse type for the immunoEM experiment was not mentioned.. Experimental description: Adult male Sprague-Dawley rats were used for immunogold staining.
